title: The One After Vegas
weakest_section: 22:00-23:00 range with fewer laughs during resolution

jokes.text: Hello, Mrs. Ross. Well, hello, Mr. Rachel.
jokes.setup: Post-wedding greeting with traditional spousal titles
jokes.punchline: Gender-swapped name combinations creating absurd formal titles
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay with good economy. The reversal works well structurally.

jokes.text: I don't think they're as much dating as they are... two bottles of vodka walking around in human form.
jokes.setup: Question about Ross and Rachel's relationship status
jokes.punchline: Creative metaphor reducing them to their intoxication level
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor with strong visual imagery. Creative way to describe extreme drunkenness that feels fresh.

jokes.text: If you marry in Vegas, you're only married in Vegas.
jokes.setup: Concern about the validity of a Vegas wedding
jokes.punchline: Completely false but confidently delivered fake rule
jokes.explanation: Plays on Vegas stereotypes but the false confidence delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: Do you have any clothes on? Yes. Really? No.
jokes.setup: Post-blackout morning confusion about their state
jokes.punchline: Quick reversal revealing the awkwardness of the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom structure with good timing. Not groundbreaking but executed well.

jokes.text: I remember laughing. I laughed a lot. Then we didn't have sex.
jokes.setup: Trying to piece together what happened the night before
jokes.punchline: Logic that laughing precludes intimacy
jokes.explanation: Character-specific logic that feels authentic to Monica's analytical nature.

jokes.text: Too depressed to eat. I'll probably eat in, like, five minutes.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he's too depressed to eat
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction showing his true priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his love of food trumps all emotions. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Are you asking me to have a 'frienaissance'?
jokes.setup: Discussion about renewing their friendship
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's made-up portmanteau word
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's very Phoebe - combining 'friend' and 'renaissance' in her unique way.

jokes.text: It's a buffet, man.
jokes.setup: Someone waiting for a waitress while starving
jokes.punchline: Simple observation that solves the 'problem'
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor. Gets a mild reaction but nothing special.

jokes.text: Why wasn't I invited? And who was gonna be your best man? Don't say Ross. Do not say Ross.
jokes.setup: Joey learning about Chandler almost getting married
jokes.punchline: Immediate shift to self-interest and competitiveness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's priorities and his rivalry with Ross for Chandler's attention.

jokes.text: Tell her she's not marriage material. Girls say it to me all the time. And if she's anything like me, she's just gonna be relieved.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Joey's terrible advice based on his own romantic failures
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey giving terrible advice while revealing his own romantic shortcomings.

jokes.text: We were just talking about bacon. No, we were talking about tennis. Tennis is more believable.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler caught talking privately
jokes.punchline: Panicked lying that gets worse with each attempt
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of panic-induced bad lying. The meta-commentary makes it work.

jokes.text: Now, I'm drunk right now. I can't have a mimosa with breakfast? I'm on vacation.
jokes.setup: Observation about Vegas wedding validity while drunk
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of current drunkenness with vacation justification
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the continued drinking problem with vacation rationalization.

jokes.text: Yeah, that guy's finally gonna be able to put in that pool.
jokes.setup: Ross needing to find a divorce lawyer
jokes.punchline: Implication that Ross's frequent divorces are profitable for his lawyer
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that cleverly references Ross's pattern of divorces through the lawyer's perspective.

jokes.text: Ross, I don't think surgery is the answer here.
jokes.setup: Ross mentioning getting an annulment
jokes.punchline: Rachel confusing annulment with medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Basic malapropism humor. Gets a reaction but feels predictable.

jokes.text: Your thing. You're the guy who gets divorced. Yes, you do. This is your third divorce. You love divorce so much, you're probably gonna marry it... and then it won't work out, so you're gonna have to divorce it.
jokes.setup: Ross denying that divorce is 'his thing'
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurd scenario about marrying divorce itself
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that gets more absurd while staying character-specific. The recursive joke about divorcing divorce is clever.

jokes.text: I'm so drunk.
jokes.setup: Rachel's elaborate divorce joke
jokes.punchline: Simple admission explaining her rambling
jokes.explanation: Perfect button to the previous joke with maximum economy. The simplicity after complexity works well.

jokes.text: Eight we get married... but 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11, 12, we don't get married.
jokes.setup: Setting up dice roll to decide marriage
jokes.punchline: Completely skewed odds heavily favoring not getting married
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's commitment fears through deliberately bad odds.

jokes.text: Last night I rolled a hard eight. It was the wrong kind of eight. No wedding! Damn it! I wanted it so bad!
jokes.setup: Rolling an eight which should mean marriage
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sudden commitment flip and fake disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the dice setup with Chandler's characteristic flip-flopping on commitment.

jokes.text: We've been on the road for 6 hours, and you've been asleep for 5 and a half.
jokes.setup: Complaint about Joey sleeping during road trip
jokes.punchline: Specific timing that shows he was asleep almost immediately
jokes.explanation: Good specificity that makes the complaint funnier. The precise timing adds to the frustration.

jokes.text: That will be your punishment, you greedy sleeper!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's anger at Joey's sleeping
jokes.punchline: Unique insult combining greed with sleeping
jokes.explanation: Very Phoebe insult that's both creative and character-specific. 'Greedy sleeper' is memorable.

jokes.text: Are my eyes open? No!
jokes.setup: Joey asking if his eyes are open while driving
jokes.punchline: Confirmation that he's driving with eyes closed
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective physical comedy setup. The danger makes it funnier.

jokes.text: And I will make everyone call me Mrs. Geller.
jokes.setup: Rachel agreeing to stay married
jokes.punchline: Taking the fake marriage too seriously
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's tendency to get carried away with ideas.

jokes.text: I thought that the boxes were far away from each other.
jokes.setup: Ross's argument about checking Miss vs Mrs boxes
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic reason for changing her mind
jokes.explanation: Good callback that deflates Ross's ridiculous argument with absurd literalism.

jokes.text: You are asking me to be your wife. And as my wife, I think you should grant me this favor.
jokes.setup: Ross asking Rachel to do him a favor
jokes.punchline: Using marriage logic to justify the marriage favor
jokes.explanation: Clever circular logic that shows Ross's manipulative tendencies. Well-structured irony.

jokes.text: Don't you think I asked him that before he got in?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's concern about picking up a potential rapist
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact approach to hitchhiker safety
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - treating a complex safety issue with childlike simplicity.

jokes.text: And, you, are you a rapist? No. Do you like car games?
jokes.setup: Phoebe questioning the hitchhiker's intentions
jokes.punchline: Immediate topic shift from serious to trivial
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition between serious safety concerns and frivolous entertainment priorities.

jokes.text: I came up with an analogy about a little goldfish... in a pond that freezes over in the winter... but I realized that was a story about what to do when people pick on you. So I'm gonna actually save that for my children's book.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to explain why they can't stay married
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong analogy leading to children's book idea
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's scattered thinking and creative tangents.

jokes.text: This is not a marriage! This is the world's worst hangover!
jokes.setup: Ross treating their situation like a real marriage
jokes.punchline: Rachel's metaphor reducing marriage to hangover consequences
jokes.explanation: Strong metaphor that perfectly captures the situation's absurdity. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Hey, hey... is there any such thing as an annulment shower?
jokes.setup: Ross agreeing to get the annulment
jokes.punchline: Applying wedding traditions to divorce proceedings
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor playing with social conventions. Gets a chuckle but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: I have your name and the fact that you're a drifter. So ball's pretty much in your court.
jokes.setup: Hitchhiker leaving with contact information exchange
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's complete lack of useful contact information
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe logic - treating an impossible situation as if it's reasonable.

jokes.text: That's not fair. You know I can't resist that beautiful voice.
jokes.setup: Joey singing 'Space Oddity' to win Phoebe over
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's inability to stay mad when he sings
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Phoebe's weakness for Joey's singing. Feels authentic.

jokes.text: So I will strive to stay awake for as long as I know you. You can still sleep at night and stuff.
jokes.setup: Joey's apology for sleeping during the road trip
jokes.punchline: Clarification that normal sleep is still allowed
jokes.explanation: Sweet Joey moment with good clarification humor. Shows his literal thinking.

jokes.text: Is it a meatball sub? That is incredible! You are the master!
jokes.setup: 20 Questions game with first guess
jokes.punchline: Joey getting it right immediately showing his food obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his food obsession makes him psychic at food-related games.

jokes.text: Don't say it! Don't even think it. Chandler, enjoy your handful.
jokes.setup: Chandler and Monica covered in bugs/moths
jokes.punchline: Monica's desperate attempt to stop inevitable sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with Monica's controlling personality trying to prevent innuendo.

jokes.text: Go unpack. It's been three days, and it drives me insane!
jokes.setup: Discussion about not wanting change
jokes.punchline: Monica's OCD taking over mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica moment where her compulsions override everything else. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Door hasn't been locked in five years, but okay.
jokes.setup: Monica giving Chandler a key to christen
jokes.punchline: Practical observation undercutting the romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - deflating romantic moments with practical observations.

jokes.text: The key broke in the lock, and I can't get in. Wait. Oh, my God, I can't get out! This isn't a sign! No, it's a very old key!
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to use the ceremonial key
jokes.punchline: Immediate mechanical failure and panic about signs
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation with callback to the signs theme. Well-executed panic.

jokes.text: Are you hugging the door right now? No. Yeah, yeah, me neither.
jokes.setup: Separation by broken door
jokes.punchline: Both clearly hugging the door while denying it
jokes.explanation: Sweet visual comedy showing their connection even through mishaps. Good economy.

jokes.text: No, thanks. I've already seen one.
jokes.setup: Invitation to see a movie
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's logic that one movie is enough for life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic with maximum economy. The absurd reasoning delivered matter-of-factly is brilliant.

jokes.text: What? We're still married. Don't tell Rachel. See you later.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he got the annulment
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of lying with conspiratorial tone
jokes.explanation: Good cliffhanger moment showing Ross's pattern of deception. Sets up future storylines effectively.

jokes.text: Nice job, Joe. You're quite the craftsman!
jokes.setup: Joey attempting to fix the broken lock
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic praise for making the situation worse
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response to incompetence. Gets a reaction but nothing special.

tmdb_overview: Ross and Rachel wake with a shocking discovery: they're married. Chandler and Monica reconsider their plans, while Phoebe and Joey return to New York.
